formData.subject: PROCUREMENT OF REPLACEMENT MOTOR TRANSPORT VEHICLES

references.title: MARCORSYSCOM Motor Transport Readiness Report dtd 15 Dec 25

paragraphs.text: Request your decision on the procurement of 24 replacement Medium Tactical Vehicle Replacement (MTVR) trucks to address critical motor transport readiness shortfalls.

Per reference (b), the Division's MTVR fleet is currently at 67% readiness, well below the 85% threshold established in reference (a). Twelve vehicles have been classified as beyond economical repair, and an additional twelve are in Condition Code H status with estimated repair costs exceeding 65% of replacement value.

**Option 1 (Recommended):** Procure 24 new MTVRs through the MARCORSYSCOM vehicle replacement program at a total cost of $7.2M. Delivery timeline: 8-10 months. This option restores fleet readiness to 91% and provides vehicles with full warranty coverage.
**Option 2:** Procure 12 new MTVRs ($3.6M) and overhaul 12 existing vehicles ($1.8M) for a total of $5.4M. This achieves 82% readiness but the overhauled vehicles will have a shorter service life.

**Option 3:** Defer procurement to FY27 and redistribute vehicles from lower-priority units. This option has no additional cost but will degrade readiness across multiple commands and is not recommended.

Option 1 provides the most cost-effective long-term solution. The cost-benefit analysis at enclosure (1) demonstrates a 23% lower total lifecycle cost compared to Option 2 over a 10-year period. Enclosure (2) provides detailed condition assessments for all affected vehicles.
Recommend approval of Option 1 to procure 24 new MTVRs. Funding is available in the FY26 Operations and Maintenance, Marine Corps (O&M,MC) account.
